title: Data Manipulation & Transactions
description: The write half of SQL: INSERT (full and column-list forms), UPDATE (one column or several, computed from the old value), the career-ending danger of an UPDATE/DELETE with no WHERE, and DELETE. Then the safety net: transactions (BEGIN, COMMIT, ROLLBACK) and atomicity, autocommit and the sharp Oracle-vs-SQLite split, and how a foreign key governs deleting a referenced row.
